title: Octets, Byte Streams, Fragmentation, Buffering, and Partial I/O · Language Lens: Buffered Reader Ownership
summary: A parser assumes one Read returns a complete line, converts arbitrary bytes to text too early, loses buffered bytes, and treats a short read or write as end-of-message. Planned artifact: Add and verify the buffered reader ownership increment in fragment-safe byte-stream reader and writer contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use bufio.Reader without losing peeked, unread, or already-buffered bytes when protocol phases change.
Complete bounded output across short writes while retaining progress, error, and ownership evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Buffered Reader Ownership · Theory Buffered Reader Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 2453 in a privacy-sensitive proxy translating between two parser implementations. Build deterministic Go evidence to preserve partial bytes while distinguishing fragmentation from incomplete input; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Buffered Reader Ownership contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Buffered Reader Ownership · Theory Buffered Reader Ownership.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use bufio.reader without losing peeked, unread, or already-buffered bytes when protocol phases change. For Predict Buffered Reader Ownership · Theory Buffered Reader Ownership, keep Buffered Reader Ownership connected to executable Go behavior evidence.
